headline: Rohingya education access limited to 120 community centres
context: Rohingya refugee children in Malaysia access education through only 120 community learning centres, serving approximately 12,000 of 45,000 school-age children.

cards.big: Only 12,000 of 45,000 Rohingya school-age children access education in Malaysia
cards.sub: 120 community centres operate without government recognition or funding

cards.big: Education Act 1996 makes primary education compulsory for Malaysian children only
cards.sub: Non-citizen children have no statutory right to attend national schools

cards.big: CRC Article 28 obligates education access for all children in a state's jurisdiction
cards.sub: Malaysia's CRC reservation does not exempt obligation to children within territory

cards.big: Community learning centre teachers earn average RM600 monthly from donations
cards.sub: Quality of education limited by resources, with no accredited curriculum

cards.big: 33,000 children without education today become 33,000 adults without opportunities
UNHCR allocated RM8.4 million for refugee education programmes in Malaysia for 2025
